summary: http-uploader-dev preinstall beacons to Burp Collaborator OAST endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` script (`bun run index.js`) detects the installer's operating system and issues `ping <platform>.prkxwye2gzh9f93cetkm3ilfm6sxgo4d.oastify.com`, where `<platform>` is `mac`, `linux`, or `win`. The DNS resolution causes the attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain to record the installer's public IP, resolver, and OS identifier. The same script also launches the local calculator (a standard proof-of-concept marker for code execution). Neither behavior relates to the package's advertised file-upload purpose. The lifecycle hook also invokes the Bun runtime rather than Node, matching the alternate-runtime invocation pattern that bypasses scanners which only inspect Node-resolved code paths.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
